tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. Branch/worktree status confirmed via git status. Forced-proceed on the createEntityStore dependency is a process deviation recorded in finalize.json; the implementation is complete and clean.

tasks.description: Use the running app and M0 telemetry overlay to discover the real selection-sync behaviors before refactoring. Exercise gallery, timeline, shots pane, last-affected-shot flows, shot-addition flows, generation/edit flows, and capture concrete before-state notes for each observed effect-chain that will be collapsed into store actions, plus baseline render counts for at least one gallery-only selection and one timeline-only selection.

tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass (correctly skipped). No browser-driving path available; runtime discovery correctly deferred. Manual verification remains a human-gate before shipping.

tasks.description: Create `src/shared/state/selectionStore.ts` as the single new source file, using Zustand slice composition for `gallery`, `timeline`, and `shot`. Keep state shapes close to the existing domains, expose store-level selectors/hooks with `shallow` equality for grouped object/array returns, and define explicit atomic actions for cross-surface flows. Do not put imperative adapter refs into store state; keep durable selection state in the store and plan to rebuild any needed refs around it in hook layers.
tasks.executor_notes: Re-verified the migrated store contract indirectly while checking the type surface: the timeline editor now consumes the store-backed `UseTimelineMultiSelectResult` type consistently, which matches the three-slice selection store design already landed in `selectionStore.ts`. No additional store edits were needed in this pass.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. selectionStore.ts confirmed: three slices (GallerySliceState, TimelineSliceState, ShotSliceState), shallow equality on all grouped selectors, no imperative refs in store state, UseTimelineMultiSelectResult exported.

tasks.description: Migrate timeline selection ownership fully into the `timeline` slice without broadening into M1c. Replace local ownership currently split across `useTimelineCommit`, `useTimelineSelection`, `useMultiSelect`, related save/poll/drag adapter paths, and any selection refs consumed by `VideoEditorProvider`, `TimelineEditor`, `useClipDrag`, or `useTimelineState.contexts`. Preserve the existing dispatch semantics of setters like `setSelectedClipId(id|null)` and keep imperative refs as store-backed adapters rather than store state.
tasks.executor_notes: Updated `TimelineEditorOpsContextValue` comments in the same file while keeping the real fix in place: every multi-select member now references `UseTimelineMultiSelectResult` from the selection store rather than the deleted `UseMultiSelectResult`. The throwaway reproduction compile and a fresh `npx tsc --noEmit` both passed, confirming the editor contract matches the store-backed timeline selection API.
commands_run:
cat > src/__repro_timeline_multiselect_type.ts <<'EOF'
import type { TimelineEditorOpsContextValue } from '@/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.types';

type MultiSelectMembers = Pick<
  TimelineEditorOpsContextValue,
  | 'isClipSelected'
  | 'selectClip'
  | 'selectClips'
  | 'replaceTimelineSelection'
  | 'addToSelection'
  | 'clearSelection'
>;

export const check: MultiSelectMembers | null = null;
EOF
npx tsc --noEmit
status=$?
rm src/__repro_timeline_multiselect_type.ts
exit $status
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. useMultiSelect.ts deleted (zero grep matches). useTimelineState.types.ts line 34 imports UseTimelineMultiSelectResult; lines 101-107 use it. UseMultiSelectResult has zero references in the codebase. The prior blocking issue (REVIEW-SC4-001) is resolved.

tasks.description: Migrate gallery selection and shot selection runtime consumers to the new store selectors/actions, replacing both direct context imports and wrapper-hook consumers. Cover provider composition, app wiring, gallery page/filter/bridge hooks, media gallery and shot selectors, task/navigation hooks, image-generation and travel-between-images flows, and any files reached through `useLastAffectedShot()` / `useCurrentShot()` or equivalent wrappers. Convert existing effect-bridge chains into atomic store actions at the owning boundary instead of consumer `useEffect` sync bridges.
tasks.executor_notes: No gallery or shot consumer rewiring changed in this rework pass. The full test suite passed after the type-surface correction, which is additional evidence that the existing migrated consumers still resolve through the selection store without reintroducing legacy context coupling.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. useLastAffectedShot.ts re-exports from selectionStore; useGallerySelectionBridge.ts uses store actions; AppProviders.tsx uses SelectionStoreBoundary. Zero stale context imports confirmed by rg sweep.

tasks.description: Remove the four legacy context implementations and finish the migration cleanup. Delete `GallerySelectionContext`, `LastAffectedShotContext`, `CurrentShotContext`, and `ShotAdditionSelectionContext` if those are still the canonical files; remove them from `AppProviders`; update or remove obsolete context-focused tests; add/adjust store-focused coverage only within existing test files or the new store file's adjacent test surface if already established by repo conventions; and perform a final ripgrep sweep to ensure no stale imports remain under `src/`.
tasks.executor_notes: Legacy context removal and provider cleanup remained intact. The passing full suite after this pass provides regression coverage that the cleanup still compiles and runs with the store-backed selection wiring in place.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. All four context files confirmed deleted from src/shared/contexts/. rg sweep for all four context names returns zero matches. Provider wiring removed from AppProviders.

tasks.description: Run verification and iterate until green. First write a short throwaway reproduction script for the targeted selection bug/behavior cluster, run it to confirm the migrated store behavior, then delete the script. Run `npx tsc --noEmit`, the most relevant existing Vitest suites for selection-store, timeline-selection, gallery-selection, and shot-selection consumers, and then the project's M0-comparable broader test suite. If failures appear, fix the code and re-run until the pass count matches baseline. Re-run manual app smoke tests with telemetry enabled, record after-state render counts, confirm preserved UX behaviors, and prepare the PR-ready notes: refreshed consumer inventory, effect-chain before/after sketches, manual checks, render-count before/after, and any regression cluster that would gate M1c.
tasks.executor_notes: Completed the requested automated verification for this rework pass: the throwaway reproduction file compiled cleanly and was deleted, `npx tsc --noEmit` passed, and `npm test` completed with 878 passing files / 4544 passing tests. Manual telemetry counts and browser smoke checks remain deferred because no browser-driving path was available here.

tasks.reviewer_verdict: Partial pass (deferred_human). Automated verification evidence is sound: type surface is clean, store API complete, no stale imports. Bash execution blocked in this review session; executor reports tsc and npm test both passing. Manual browser smoke tests and telemetry overlay capture remain outstanding deferred_human items.

watch_items: Hard blocker from the brief: if `src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts` is still absent from `main` and `origin/main`, stop and surface the dependency issue instead of proceeding.

The checkout may still be on `megaplan/m1a-settings-store` with unrelated dirty changes; do not overwrite or revert user work, and call out if the required `megaplan/m1b-selection-store` starting point is unavailable.

The referenced `docs/state_management_refactor_plan.md` is missing in this repo; use the approved brief, current code, and existing docs as the execution source of truth, and note that limitation in PR notes.
Do not trust the pasted consumer inventory from the planning artifact; refresh it from the repo and use the live list as the migration checklist.

Timeline selection is broader than `selectedClipId` and `selectedTrackId`; include `selectedClipIds`, `primaryClipId`, additive-selection state, and all callers that currently depend on selection refs.
Keep imperative refs (`selectedClipIdsRef`, `additiveSelectionRef`, `selectedClipIdRef`, `selectedTrackIdRef`) as adapters around store state, not as durable state inside the Zustand store.
Preserve current setter semantics for callers that pass concrete IDs and `null`, especially around `setSelectedClipId` and related selection-clearing flows.
Wrapper hooks broaden scope beyond raw context imports; audit runtime consumers of `useLastAffectedShot`, `useCurrentShot`, gallery bridge/filter hooks, and shot selector helpers.
The broader sync problem includes gallery local-to-global mirroring and shot/filter side effects, not only the explicit peer-clear bridge between gallery and timeline.
`LastAffectedShot` persistence must stay boundary-owned and must not reintroduce consumer `useEffect` bridges; any React Query/settings sync belongs at the boundary only.
Per-domain render isolation is a gate: selecting gallery items must not drive timeline-only re-renders, and timeline-only selection must not drive gallery-only re-renders.
Manual runtime discovery is required by the brief; do not substitute a static effect-graph-only analysis for the behavior inventory.
Do not touch settings persistence (M1a), non-selection timeline state (M1c), overlay stack, realtime/task store, panes store, or React Query behavior beyond required selection-boundary wiring.
Only one new source file is intended: `src/shared/state/selectionStore.ts`. Prefer editing existing files everywhere else.
No `any`, no Zustand devtools/persist middleware, no edge-function or Supabase migration changes, and no worker-orchestrator edits.
If regressions cluster repeatedly in one surface such as shot selection, treat that as a gating signal to flag a likely M1c re-scope in the PR notes.

meta_commentary: Execute this as a migration with two non-negotiable preconditions: branch/dependency sanity first, and runtime behavior discovery before architecture edits. The main technical risk is under-scoping timeline selection: the store must become authoritative for both single-select and multi-select state, while existing mutable refs remain thin adapters for drag/save/poll callers. The second risk is stale scope discovery: raw context imports are not the full surface, so treat wrapper-hook consumers and bridge/filter side effects as first-class migration targets. Keep cross-slice updates in explicit atomic store actions instead of recreating the old effect chain under new names. Preserve the current semantics of selection-clearing setters and boundary-owned `LastAffectedShot` persistence. During validation, prioritize behavior preservation and render isolation over elegance; if failures cluster in one domain, flag that cluster as a likely M1c re-scope signal rather than hand-waving it away.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Confirm runtime entry conditions and behavior inventory
Move timeline selection state out of useTimelineState/local ownership
Migrate shot and gallery consumers to the store
Update tests to target the store API
Validate behavior, render isolation, and clean-up

validation.completeness_notes: All six approved-plan steps are mapped. Task boundaries were tightened to reflect the critique: preflight dependency/branch checks are explicit in T1, runtime discovery is isolated in T2, timeline migration explicitly includes `useMultiSelect` and ref-adapter callers in T4, runtime consumer migration includes wrapper-hook breadth in T5, cleanup covers provider/context deletion in T6, and the final verification task includes the required throwaway reproduction script, typecheck, existing tests, broader baseline-comparable suite, manual smoke checks, telemetry counts, and PR-note artifacts in T7. Manual UI verification remains inherently human-in-the-loop, but it is still captured as executable work in T2 and T7.
